coreTruthSet.description: The mother’s mediating authority is conveyed through physical placement optimized for bilateral listening and non-dominating influence.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: In the place where someone could hear both sides without turning.

coreTruthSet.description: A minimal embodied intervention by the mother changes interaction dynamics without overt command or volume increase.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: She simply adjusted her position... and in that small movement, something changed.

coreTruthSet.description: The focal child’s observer positioning is framed as intentional edge-of-circle learning rather than formal inclusion.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Not close enough to be called. Not far enough to miss anything.

coreTruthSet.description: Shared bundle placement functions as a procedural object that delays individual claim until relational timing is collectively acceptable.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: A bundle lay between them... placed where everyone could see it and no one could claim it until the moment was right.

coreTruthSet.description: De-escalation occurs through reframing language that widens responsibility attribution instead of escalating direct blame.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: The words moved through the group... not as a challenge. As a widening.

settingAnchors.name: Firelight Council Edge
settingAnchors.description: Night mediation setting outside residential cluster where low-volume bilateral dispute handling and procedural object placement occur.

continuityConstraints.title: Non-Forcing Mediation Technique
continuityConstraints.text: Effective mediation can proceed through breath pacing, silence, and position rather than overt verbal dominance.

continuityConstraints.title: Face Reading as Truth Channel
continuityConstraints.text: In high-tension negotiation scenes, face response and body shift are primary truth channels when explicit speech is partial or strategic.

continuityConstraints.title: Observer-to-Bridge Formation
continuityConstraints.text: Bridge leadership emerges from repeated edge-position observation of procedural, emotional, and relational dynamics before formal authority transfer.

openResearchGaps.title: What social thresholds distinguish tolerated observer presence from prohibited participation for children in council-like settings?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to preserve realism in repeated observer-learning beats.

openResearchGaps.title: What procedural sequence governs who speaks, when objects are acknowledged, and when settlement terms become claimable?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to keep future mediation scenes legally and culturally coherent.

openResearchGaps.title: Which silence and positional cues are culturally legible as de-escalation signals across groups?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed for consistent nonverbal conflict-resolution writing across chapters.
